ogTitle: Lake Haven vs Lake Hume Village: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Lake Haven and Lake Hume Village.
heading: Lake Haven vs Lake Hume Village — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Lake Haven, New South Wales and Lake Hume Village, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Lake Hume Village has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to Lake Haven (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Lake Haven or Lake Hume Village safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Lake Haven has a flood risk score of 23/100, while Lake Hume Village scores 42/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Lake Haven scores 35/100 for bushfire risk. Lake Hume Village scores 37/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Lake Haven or Lake Hume Village?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Lake Hume Village has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.63% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
